page.title: Marbella Jazz Festival 2026: Yacht Charter Experience

data.meta_description: Book a Marbella Jazz Festival 2026 yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Enjoy live jazz, sunset cruises & luxury boats from €749/2h. Reserve now.
data.summary: Experience the Marbella Jazz Festival 2026 from the water with a private yacht charter. Depart from Puerto Banús on luxury boats starting at €749/2h, with skipper, drinks, and snacks included.
key_facts: Marbella Jazz Festival 2026 runs in late June/early July at venues like Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina.
Charter a yacht from Puerto Banús for a floating concert experience with onboard sound systems.
Prices start at €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39) including skipper, drinks, and VAT.
Mangusta 80 yachts (24m, 12 pax) available from €4,719 for 4 hours, with a jet ski included free.
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, snacks, and insurance.
Sunset jazz cruises are popular—book early to secure your preferred boat and time slot.
Nearby anchorages: Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and Cabopino offer calm waters for drifting to live jazz.
Private charters can be tailored with catering, DJ, or extra hours via WhatsApp.

faq.q: What is the Marbella Jazz Festival?
faq.a: The Marbella Jazz Festival is an annual summer event featuring international and Spanish jazz artists. Concerts are held at iconic locations including Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, and the Old Town. The 2026 edition is expected in late June to early July, with a mix of free and ticketed performances.

faq.q: Can I watch jazz performances from a yacht?
faq.a: Yes. Many festival stages are set up near the water in Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina. By chartering a yacht, you can anchor close to the action, enjoy the music from your deck, and avoid crowds. Boats like the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80 offer spacious sundecks perfect for a floating concert.

faq.q: Which yachts are best for a jazz festival cruise?
faq.a: For small groups, the Astondoa 40 (9 pax) or Azimut 39 (11 pax) are ideal at €749/2h. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (12 pax) includes a free jet ski and costs €4,719 for 4 hours. The Ferretti 94 (12 pax) offers luxury for a premium experience. All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden fees. You only need to bring your own music playlist or request a DJ via WhatsApp.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for the Marbella Jazz Festival?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. Specify your preferred boat, date, and time. For boats not listed with fixed rates (e.g., Ferretti 94, Pershing 46), we provide a custom quote. Early booking is recommended as festival dates sell out quickly.

faq.q: Can I add extra services like catering or a DJ?
faq.a: Absolutely. We can arrange catering, a private DJ, or additional hours. Just let us know your requirements via WhatsApp. We also offer custom itineraries including sunset cruises to Cala Cortés or Cabopino.

faq.q: What is the best time to charter for the jazz festival?
faq.a: Evening charters (6 PM to 10 PM) are popular for sunset jazz cruises. The weather in Marbella during late June averages 28°C, with calm seas. A 4-hour charter from 6 PM allows you to enjoy the sunset and catch the main evening concert from the water.

faq.q: Is there parking near Puerto Banús for charter guests?
faq.a: Puerto Banús has several paid parking garages (approx. €2–3/hour). We recommend arriving 30 minutes before departure. Alternatively, taxis and ride-shares drop off directly at the port entrance. Our meeting point is at the charter desk near the main entrance.

data.body_html:
The Marbella Jazz Festival 2026 returns to the Costa del Sol, transforming Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina into open-air jazz venues. For the ultimate experience, combine world-class music with a private Marbella Jazz Festival yacht charter. Drift offshore, sip cava, and listen to live saxophone as the sun sets over La Concha. Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús, with prices starting at €749 for two hours on an Astondoa 40. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance. No hidden fees. Just you, your guests, and the music.

Why charter a yacht for the Marbella Jazz Festival?

Watching jazz from a yacht gives you the best seat in the house—literally on the water. You avoid crowded terraces, enjoy your own sound system, and can anchor near the stage at Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina. The festival typically runs from late June to early July, with evening concerts that coincide with the golden hour. A yacht charter in Marbella lets you combine the festival with a sunset cruise to Cala del Faro or Cabopino. Temperatures hover around 28°C, with light breezes—perfect for alfresco jazz.

Our fleet for jazz festival charters

We offer a range of boats suitable for couples, families, and groups up to 12 guests. All depart from Puerto Banús. Below are our fixed-rate tiers—other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

BoatLengthMax GuestsPrice (2h / 8h)Notes
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749 / €2,299Ideal for couples or small groups
Azimut 3912.5m11€749 / €2,299Spacious flybridge for sunset views
Mangusta 80 (Grey/White)24m12€4,719 (min 4h)Includes free jet ski

For larger parties, the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) or Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offer luxury amenities. Contact us via WhatsApp for custom quotes on these and other boats like the Pershing 46 or Azimut 58 Flybridge.

What's included in every charter?

  • Licensed skipper (English & Spanish speaking)
  • Fuel for the full charter duration
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (olives, crisps, nuts)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21% VAT)

No extra costs. You can bring your own playlist or request a DJ via WhatsApp. For a premium touch, ask about catering with local seafood or tapas.

Best anchorages for jazz festival cruises

During the festival, we recommend these spots for anchoring and listening to live music drifting from shore:

  • Puerto Banús outer breakwater – close to the main stage, calm waters
  • Marbella Marina – near the Old Town concerts
  • Cala del Faro – a quiet cove 15 minutes west, ideal for sunset jazz
  • Cala Cortés – secluded, with crystal-clear water for swimming between sets
  • Cabopino – 20 minutes east, with a relaxed beach bar vibe

All are within a 30-minute cruise from Puerto Banús. Your skipper will navigate to the best spot based on wind and crowd conditions.

Sample itinerary: Evening jazz charter

Duration: 4 hours (6 PM – 10 PM)
Boat: Azimut 39 (11 pax) – €1,499 (4h rate on request)
Route: Depart Puerto Banús → cruise west along the Golden Mile → anchor at Cala del Faro for sunset → return to Puerto Banús for the main concert → enjoy jazz from the water → dock at 10 PM.

This itinerary includes drinks, snacks, and a stop for a swim if time allows. For a longer experience, extend to 8 hours and include dinner at a beach club like Ocean Club Marbella.

How to book your Marbella Jazz Festival yacht

Reserve via WhatsApp with your preferred boat, date, and time. For boats with fixed rates (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, Mangusta 80), we can confirm availability within minutes. For other yachts, we'll provide a custom quote. Browse all Marbella yacht charters for more options. Early booking is essential—festival dates fill up weeks in advance, especially for sunset slots.

Frequently asked questions

What is the Marbella Jazz Festival?

The Marbella Jazz Festival is an annual summer event featuring international and Spanish jazz artists. Concerts are held at iconic locations including Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, and the Old Town. The 2026 edition is expected in late June to early July, with a mix of free and ticketed performances.

Can I watch jazz performances from a yacht?

Yes. Many festival stages are set up near the water in Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina. By chartering a yacht, you can anchor close to the action, enjoy the music from your deck, and avoid crowds. Boats like the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80 offer spacious sundecks perfect for a floating concert.

Which yachts are best for a jazz festival cruise?

For small groups, the Astondoa 40 (9 pax) or Azimut 39 (11 pax) are ideal at €749/2h. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (12 pax) includes a free jet ski and costs €4,719 for 4 hours. The Ferretti 94 (12 pax) offers luxury for a premium experience. All depart from Puerto Banús.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden fees. You only need to bring your own music playlist or request a DJ via WhatsApp.

How do I book a yacht for the Marbella Jazz Festival?

Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. Specify your preferred boat, date, and time. For boats not listed with fixed rates (e.g., Ferretti 94, Pershing 46), we provide a custom quote. Early booking is recommended as festival dates sell out quickly.

Can I add extra services like catering or a DJ?

Absolutely. We can arrange catering, a private DJ, or additional hours. Just let us know your requirements via WhatsApp. We also offer custom itineraries including sunset cruises to Cala Cortés or Cabopino.

What is the best time to charter for the jazz festival?

Evening charters (6 PM to 10 PM) are popular for sunset jazz cruises. The weather in Marbella during late June averages 28°C, with calm seas. A 4-hour charter from 6 PM allows you to enjoy the sunset and catch the main evening concert from the water.

Is there parking near Puerto Banús for charter guests?

Puerto Banús has several paid parking garages (approx. €2–3/hour). We recommend arriving 30 minutes before departure. Alternatively, taxis and ride-shares drop off directly at the port entrance. Our meeting point is at the charter desk near the main entrance.
